MB_MaterialAndUVRect Class Reference

Public Member Functions

 MB_MaterialAndUVRect (Material mat, Rect destRect, bool allPropsUseSameTiling, Rect sourceMaterialTiling, Rect samplingEncapsulatingRect, Rect srcUVsamplingRect, MB_TextureTilingTreatment treatment, string objName)
 
override int GetHashCode ()
 
override bool Equals (object obj)
 
Rect GetEncapsulatingRect ()
 
Rect GetMaterialTilingRect ()
 

Public Attributes

Material material
 The source material that was baked into the atlas.
 
Rect atlasRect
 The addressables primary key for this material at bake time. DECIDED NOT TO USE THIS BECAUSE THERE IS NOT EDITOR API FOR GETTING ADDRESS.
 
string srcObjName
 For debugging. The name of the first srcObj that uses this MaterialAndUVRect.
 
int textureArraySliceIdx = -1
 
bool allPropsUseSameTiling = true
 True if the albedoMap, normalMap, specMap, etc... all use the same tiling.
 
Rect allPropsUseSameTiling_sourceMaterialTiling
 Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = true. Else should be 0,0,0,0 The material tiling on the source material.
 
Rect allPropsUseSameTiling_samplingEncapsulatinRect
 Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = true. Else should be 0,0,0,0 The encapsulating sampling rect that was used to sample for the atlas. Note that the case of dont-considerMeshUVs is the same as do-considerMeshUVs where the uv rect is 0,0,1,1.
 
Rect propsUseDifferntTiling_srcUVsamplingRect
 Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = false. The UVrect of the source mesh that was baked. We are using a trick here. Instead of storing the material tiling for each texture property here, we instead bake all those tilings into the atlases and here we pretend that all those tilings were 0,0,1,1. Then all we need is to store is the srcUVsamplingRect.
 
List< GameObject > objectsThatUse
 
MB_TextureTilingTreatment tilingTreatment = MB_TextureTilingTreatment.unknown
 The tiling type for this rectangle in the atlas. Usually use: considerUVs if we baked with the considerUVs feature none if we didn't bake with the considerUVs feature the edge to edge options are for atlases that stretch from one edge to the other with no padding (can be tiled)

)
Parameters
matThe Material
destRectThe rect in the atlas this material maps to
allPropsUseSameTilingIf true then use sourceMaterialTiling and samplingEncapsulatingRect. if false then use srcUVsamplingRect. None used values should be 0,0,0,0.
sourceMaterialTilingallPropsUseSameTiling_sourceMaterialTiling
samplingEncapsulatingRectallPropsUseSameTiling_samplingEncapsulatinRect
srcUVsamplingRectpropsUseDifferntTiling_srcUVsamplingRect

Member Data Documentation

◆ allPropsUseSameTiling

bool MB_MaterialAndUVRect.allPropsUseSameTiling = true

True if the albedoMap, normalMap, specMap, etc... all use the same tiling.

◆ allPropsUseSameTiling_samplingEncapsulatinRect

Rect MB_MaterialAndUVRect.allPropsUseSameTiling_samplingEncapsulatinRect

Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = true. Else should be 0,0,0,0 The encapsulating sampling rect that was used to sample for the atlas. Note that the case of dont-considerMeshUVs is the same as do-considerMeshUVs where the uv rect is 0,0,1,1.

◆ allPropsUseSameTiling_sourceMaterialTiling

Rect MB_MaterialAndUVRect.allPropsUseSameTiling_sourceMaterialTiling

Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = true. Else should be 0,0,0,0 The material tiling on the source material.

◆ atlasRect

Rect MB_MaterialAndUVRect.atlasRect

The addressables primary key for this material at bake time. DECIDED NOT TO USE THIS BECAUSE THERE IS NOT EDITOR API FOR GETTING ADDRESS.

The runtime material that corresponds to the bake time material. The user may be baking textures in the editor and baking meshes at runtime. Materials may at runtime loaded from asset bundles which are a different instance of the Material than the baked asset We use the Addressables address (cached at texture bake time) to look up the runtime material. DECIDED NOT TO USE THIS BECAUSE THERE IS NOT EDITOR API FOR GETTING ADDRESS.

[System.NonSerialized]

The rectangle in the atlas where the texture (including all tiling) was copied to.

◆ material

Material MB_MaterialAndUVRect.material

The source material that was baked into the atlas.

◆ objectsThatUse

List<GameObject> MB_MaterialAndUVRect.objectsThatUse

◆ propsUseDifferntTiling_srcUVsamplingRect

Rect MB_MaterialAndUVRect.propsUseDifferntTiling_srcUVsamplingRect

Only valid if allPropsUseSameTiling = false. The UVrect of the source mesh that was baked. We are using a trick here. Instead of storing the material tiling for each texture property here, we instead bake all those tilings into the atlases and here we pretend that all those tilings were 0,0,1,1. Then all we need is to store is the srcUVsamplingRect.

◆ srcObjName

string MB_MaterialAndUVRect.srcObjName

For debugging. The name of the first srcObj that uses this MaterialAndUVRect.

◆ textureArraySliceIdx

int MB_MaterialAndUVRect.textureArraySliceIdx = -1

◆ tilingTreatment

MB_TextureTilingTreatment MB_MaterialAndUVRect.tilingTreatment = MB_TextureTilingTreatment.unknown

The tiling type for this rectangle in the atlas. Usually use: considerUVs if we baked with the considerUVs feature none if we didn't bake with the considerUVs feature the edge to edge options are for atlases that stretch from one edge to the other with no padding (can be tiled)
